summary: Tutte's 3-flow conjecture (every 4-edge-connected graph has a nowhere-zero 3-flow) remains open. Thomassen (2012) resolved the long-standing weak 3-flow conjecture of Jaeger by proving every 8-edge-connected graph admits a nowhere-zero 3-flow; Lovász, Thomassen, Wu, and Zhang (2013) subsequently sharpened this to every 6-edge-connected graph being $\mathbb{Z}_3$-connected. The gap from 4-edge-connected to 6-edge-connected remains unresolved, though the conjecture has also been confirmed for several restricted graph classes including toroidal graphs (Li et al., 2022) and projective planar graphs.

since_posted.title: The weak 3-flow conjecture and the weak circular flow conjecture
since_posted.venue: Journal of Combinatorial Theory, Series B
since_posted.claim: Proves Jaeger's weak 3-flow conjecture by showing every 8-edge-connected graph is $\mathbb{Z}_3$-connected and hence admits a nowhere-zero 3-flow.

since_posted.title: Nowhere-zero 3-flows and modulo k-orientations
since_posted.authors: László Miklós Lovász, Carsten Thomassen, Yezhou Wu, Cun-Quan Zhang
since_posted.venue: Journal of Combinatorial Theory, Series B
since_posted.claim: Improves Thomassen's bound: every 6-edge-connected graph is $\mathbb{Z}_3$-connected and thus admits a nowhere-zero 3-flow, as the special case $k=3$ of a result on modulo $k$-orientations.

since_posted.title: A note on nowhere-zero 3-flow and Z_3-connectivity
since_posted.claim: Proves Tutte's 3-flow conjecture holds for every 4-edge-connected graph with at most seven 5-edge-cuts, contributing partial results toward the conjecture.

search_queries: weak 3-flow conjecture Jaeger nowhere-zero k-edge-connected solved
Thomassen "The weak 3-flow conjecture and the weak circular flow conjecture" JCTB 2012
"nowhere-zero 3-flows in toroidal graphs" authors year journal 2021
arXiv "nowhere-zero 3-flows" toroidal graphs Li Ma Miao Shi Wang Zhang preprint
notes: The toroidal graphs result (Li, Ma, Miao, Shi, Wang, Zhang, J. Comb. Theory Ser. B 153, 2022, pp. 61–80) was consistently reported in search results but could not be verified via WebFetch (ScienceDirect returned HTTP 403) and no arXiv preprint was found; it is therefore omitted from since_posted. Kochol's 2001 reduction (sufficiency of 5-edge-connected) predates the 2007 posting date and was not included. The current state-of-the-art lower bound is 6-edge-connectivity (LTWZ 2013); closing the gap to 4-edge-connectivity remains the principal open challenge.
